name: Niamh of the Golden Hair
description: Niamh Chinn Óir, meaning Niamh of the Golden Hair, is a prominent figure in Irish mythology, particularly associated with the tales of Tír na nÓg, the Land of Eternal Youth. She is often depicted as a beautiful fairy queen or princess from this otherworld realm. Niamh is best known for her role in bringing the mortal hero Oisín to Tír na nÓg. Entranced by her beauty and the promise of a blissful existence, Oisín willingly left the mortal world behind, riding with Niamh on her magical white horse across the sea. He spent what he believed to be only a few years in Tír na nÓg, experiencing joy and wonder. However, upon his return to Ireland, he discovered that centuries had passed, and his former companions were long gone. The tale of Niamh and Oisín serves as a poignant reminder of the allure of the otherworld and the inevitable passage of time in the mortal realm. Niamh embodies the beauty and enchantment of the fairy realm, and her story explores themes of love, loss, and the consequences of choosing immortality over mortality. Her golden hair is a symbol of her otherworldly beauty and power, captivating all who behold her.
subtitle: Queen of Tír na nÓg's Golden Charm
achievements: Brought Oisín to Tír na nÓg
Ruled Tír na nÓg with grace
Embodies the beauty of the Otherworld
Maintained the peace and prosperity of her realm
companions: Other fairies of Tír na nÓg

enemies.name: Time itself (in relation to Oisín's return)

keyMyths.title: Oisín's Journey
keyMyths.description: Niamh invites Oisín to Tír na nÓg, promising him eternal youth and happiness. He accepts, riding away with her on a magical horse, unaware of the passage of time in the mortal world.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of the Oisín saga

keyMyths.title: Return to Ireland
keyMyths.description: After what he believes to be a few years, Oisín returns to Ireland to find his companions. Niamh warns him not to touch the ground of Ireland, but he disobeys and instantly ages and dies.
keyMyths.source: Various versions of the Oisín saga

keyMyths.title: Golden Hair Symbolism
keyMyths.description: Niamh's golden hair represents her otherworldly beauty and power, captivating mortals and fairies alike. It is a visual representation of the allure of Tír na nÓg and the promise of eternal youth.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
sources.description: A collection of Irish myths and legends.
sources.text: A collection of Irish myths and legends.

sources.author: W.B. Yeats
sources.description: A poem based on the legend of Oisín and Niamh.
sources.text: A poem based on the legend of Oisín and Niamh.
